content: If we re-conceptualize biological pathogenic agents from the original Merriam-Webster and biological definition of 'agent' through the lens of the 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism for autonomous reinforcement learning agents, we can hypothesize that successful pathogens evolve their own intrinsic curiosity-driven exploration strategy, where they receive an 'internal evolutionary reward' for exploring novel uncolonized host niches rather than only relying on external selection pressure to drive adaptation. This framework re-frames pathogenic spread as an active, intrinsically motivated exploration process rather than a passive random diffusion.
